examples.title: Process Creation with fork()
examples.description: Demonstrates basic process control system call - creating a new process
examples.explanation: The fork() system call creates a new process (child). It returns child's PID to parent, 0 to child, and -1 on error. This demonstrates process control system calls.

examples.title: File Operations with System Calls
examples.description: Demonstrates file management system calls in Python using low-level I/O
examples.code:
import os

# Open file - corresponds to open() system call
fd = os.open("example.txt", os.O_CREAT | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)

# Write to file - corresponds to write() system call
message = b"Hello, OS System Calls!"
os.write(fd, message)

# Close file - corresponds to close() system call
os.close(fd)

# Read from file
fd = os.open("example.txt", os.O_RDONLY)
data = os.read(fd, 100)
os.close(fd)

print("File content:", data.decode())
examples.explanation: Python's os module provides direct wrappers around system calls. os.open(), os.write(), os.read(), and os.close() map directly to their respective system calls.

examples.title: Inter-Process Communication with pipe()
examples.description: Demonstrates communication system call for creating pipes between processes
examples.explanation: pipe() system call creates a unidirectional communication channel. Parent writes to pipefd[1], child reads from pipefd[0]. This demonstrates IPC system calls.

examples.title: Information Maintenance System Call
examples.description: Demonstrates getting process information using system calls
examples.explanation: This demonstrates information maintenance system calls: getpid(), getppid(), getuid(), getgid(), and getrusage() which retrieve system and process information.
